Ntc Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery
Off Calendar Mar-07-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES and the TENTATIVE RULING is ADOPTED. Defendant Irena Meshman's Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Written Discovery is granted in part and moot in part. The motion is granted as all general objections are overruled. The reservation of a right to amend the responses is ordered stricken. The procedures for amending responses to interrogatories is set forth in C.C.P. Section 2030.310.
The motion is moot as supplemental responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those supplemental responses on this motion, except as to those matters ruled upon as they have been repeated in any supplemental response. However, counsel are advised that objections to any request for documents or interrogatory as calling for information or documents equally available to defendant would be overruled. Additionally, documents are not limited to paper documents.
All requests for sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-07-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES and the TENTATIVE RULING is ADOPTED. Plaintiff Jacob Khazanovich's Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part. The form interrogatories were served prior to the filing of the cross-complaint. Moving party asserts that these form interrogatories are directed to the incident described in the cross-complaint. There is no basis for a finding that the interrogatories, which were served prior to the filing of a cross-complaint, were directed to the allegations of that cross-complaint. Had the interrogatories been served after the filing and service of the cross-complaint, the objections to incident would be improper. Plaintiff must re-serve the form interrogatories upon Defendant and Cross-Complainant with a definition that propounds the interrogatories as to the cross-complaint.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 15.1 and 17.1 as each response is inadequate. Number 17.1 is not properly answered by the response to the Request For Admission.
All requests for sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
